capabilities: Ensure secure and efficient data handling in Ethereum smart contracts using constant-time comparison and key-based partitioning.

procedure.action: Invoke constant-time comparison using bitwise operations to prevent timing attacks in Solidity following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

Invoke keccak256 to hash keys for partitioning data, ensuring even distribution across storage slots by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Release a test contract on a local Ethereum testnet and execute comparison functions with varying inputs using blue-green deployment strategy; run smoke_test() against new instance before switching traffic; assert smoke_test_pass_rate >= 1.0 before promoting.
Run gas analysis using Remix IDE to ensure constant-time operations do not exceed gas limits.
Set up automated tests using Truffle to verify partitioning logic and comparison accuracy under load.

summary: Implement constant-time secret comparison in Solidity contracts to enhance security, combined with key-based data partitioning for scalable query pruning.
title: Constant-Time Secret Comparison in Solidity with Key-Based Data Partitioning

failureConditions: Timing attacks expose secret data due to non-constant-time operations.

Inefficient data partitioning leads to uneven storage utilization; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Excessive gas usage due to complex comparison logic.
metrics: test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
successConditions: Execute comparison functions with constant gas usage regardless of input size; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Verify even distribution of data across partitions using testnet analytics; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Pass all automated tests for comparison accuracy and partitioning logic; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
